title: Neues Museum Berlin - Quick Summary
items: **The Neues Museum opened in 1859:** it was the second museum on today's Museum Island and a major Prussian cultural statement.
**World War II left it badly damaged:** the building was hit in 1943/45 and remained a ruin for decades in East Berlin.
**The restoration kept the scars visible:** David Chipperfield's 2009 reopening mixed surviving fabric with clearly modern new material.
**Nefertiti is the star:** the bust was found in 1912, arrived in Berlin in 1913 and returned to the Neues Museum in 2009.
**It is worth seeing before you go inside:** the exterior and staircase explain Berlin's approach to memory, damage and repair.
